title: bertiera subsessilis var. congolana
intro: Bertiera subsessilis var. congolana, also known as Congo bertiera, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and preventing infections, and has been used to alleviate symptoms of malaria and other diseases. Bertiera subsessilis var. congolana is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, where the leaves are boiled in water to extract its active compounds, which are then consumed for medicinal purposes.
